title: fraxinus caroliniana
intro: Fraxinus caroliniana, also known as the Carolina ash, is a deciduous tree native to the southeastern United States. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Fraxinus caroliniana include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The herbal preparations of this plant include making a tea by infusing the bark or leaves in hot water, or using it in topical creams and salves to apply directly to the skin.
